Clicking on the left charcoal square center at [34, 13] added a second white pixel to the bottom bar (now 2 W's at the right end). The game objects didn't change - only the bottom bar advanced.
So far:
- Click 1 on W center [31,19]: bottom bar +1 W
- Click 2 on B center [31,28]: centers changed (W→c, B→W), bottom bar unchanged
- Click 3 on left charcoal center [34,13]: bottom bar +1 W (now 2 W's)
It seems like clicking on different objects advances the bottom bar. The goal is probably to fill the entire bottom bar (64 pixels). Let me try clicking on the right charcoal square center and the purple bar to see which clicks advance the bar.
Actually, wait - I should be more systematic. Let me check if there's a pattern to which clicks advance the bar vs which change game objects. Maybe the goal is to click all the "centers" of all objects.
Let me try clicking on the right charcoal square center at [28, 46].

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on right charcoal square center at [28, 46]
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 28, 'col': 46}])
print("After clicking right charcoal center:")
seg = current_frame.segmentation
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
bottom = lines[63]
print(f"Bottom bar W count: {bottom.count('W')}")
print(f"Bottom: {repr(bottom)}")

# Check if any game objects changed
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] <= 20:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, at {n['boundary'][0]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
